mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market and strong consumer confidence, which translates into higher corporate revenues and profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Historically, strong employment growth correlates with economic expansion and equity market gains.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative or flat during periods of sustained PAYEMS growth (e.g., > 0.1% MoM for 3 consecutive months) in more than 50% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds a specific threshold, it signals an aggressive monetary tightening stance by the central bank, which typically dampens economic activity and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Central banks raise rates to combat inflation, often slowing growth and impacting equity valuations.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where FEDFUNDS is above 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: High and accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with slowing retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a stagflationary environment where consumer purchasing power is eroded and demand is weakening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate revenues decline.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annual change > 5% AND RSAFS 3-month average growth < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:Stagflationary environments are historically challenging for equities due to margin compression and demand destruction.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 30% of instances where CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 5% and RSAFS 3-month average growth is negative.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction (negative GDPC1 growth), a rebound in housing starts (HOUST) signals a potential bottoming out of the cycle and future economic recovery, which is bullish for equities as it implies future demand and investment.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annual growth < 0% AND HOUST 3-month average growth > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are often considered a leading indicator for economic cycles.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where GDPC1 is contracting and HOUST shows significant positive momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, which implies reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Rising unemployment is a classic recessionary signal, often preceding declines in equity markets.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust manufacturing activity and business investment, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key gauge of the health of the manufacturing sector and broader economy.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative during periods of sustained INDPRO growth (e.g., > 0.2% MoM for 3 consecutive months) in more than 50% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes corporate margins and consumer purchasing power, forcing the Federal Reserve to maintain a hawkish stance, which is detrimental to equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation typically leads to higher interest rates and lower equity multiples as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 35% of periods where CPIAUCSL annual change exceeds 7%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong retail sales (RSAFS) and low unemployment (UNRATE) suggests robust consumer confidence and spending capacity, which is a strong tailwind for corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer strength drives a large portion of the economy and corporate profits.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 30% of instances where RSAFS shows strong growth and UNRATE remains below 4.0%.
